facts.text: The First Congregational Church on Hillside Road, founded by English Puritan descendants in 1740, is Chester’s oldest standing building.
Chester’s railroad, opened in 1869, once hauled ore from local mines to market, shaping the borough’s early industrial boom.
The Chester Library’s Local History Department now hosts 392 digitized images of historic Chester, making the past accessible online.
Many 1800‑s structures in Historic Main Street survive today as restaurants, galleries, and shops, preserving Chester’s architectural heritage.
Before European settlement, the Lenni‑Lenape Indians arrived from eastern Long Island in the 1730s, founding the area that became Chester.
Actor James Gandolfini, known for “The Sopranos,” was born in Chester, adding a touch of Hollywood to the borough’s history.
